Anatta Nergui
Home Browse Graph Lexicon Cloud Index Length About
← Collection

Products And Services

What can the body
Ever want
From another body

Comfort
Necessities for daily living
Perhaps, even sympathy —
A shoulder
A listening ear
A touch

But really —
What can I — this body —
Ever want from another
Other than
Goods and postures

Is there anything more —
Than products and services
That make my life
Comfortable
Make my living
Possible

The other’s death does not concern me
So long as it does not
Disrupt my comfort
Or threaten
My living

And just for that —
There’s an entire world
Of relationships
Civilisations, wars and fear

Just for the needs
Of the body —
The body whose fate

Is doomed

Atmosphere
mordant
Crux
transaction
Words
107
Domain
relationality
Ground
reductive
Related poems
